entries.content: U.S. real GDP from MeasuringWorth.com, covering 1790-2010. MeasuringWorth provides historical GDP estimates in constant dollars, constructed from multiple underlying sources including Kuznets, Balke-Gordon, and official BEA NIPA data where available. The series is used as published without additional splicing; it provides the longest continuous GDP series for the United States.

MeasuringWorth.com. Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of the United States. Real GDP in constant dollars. Coverage 1780-2000 in original Shaikh data; extension from MeasuringWorth USGDP (2017 dollars) continues through 2024. Same source throughout—direct continuation at splice year.

Fig 2.3 displays U.S. real GDP from 1790 to present using MeasuringWorth data. The chart illustrates long-run economic growth, with exponential expansion after the Civil War and acceleration in the 20th century. Used to establish the trend context for turbulent macro dynamics discussed in Chapter 2.

citations.full: MeasuringWorth. Gross Domestic Product of the United States, 1790-Present. https://www.measuringworth.com/datasets/usgdp/
MeasuringWorth. Sources and Methods for U.S. GDP. https://www.measuringworth.com/sources.php

methodology_summary: S003 uses MeasuringWorth.com real GDP for the United States, 1790-2010. MeasuringWorth synthesizes historical GDP estimates from Kuznets, Balke-Gordon, and BEA NIPA into a single long-run series. No splicing of different sources—single provider throughout. Extension continues from same source (MeasuringWorth USGDP in 2017 dollars) with re-indexing at overlap year.
